channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Net neutrality never stood a chance
item.description: A federal appeals court sealed the fate of the Biden administration’s efforts to regulate internet providers on Thursday, striking down the Federal Communications Commission’s 2024 net neutrality order once and for all.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Pinterest's Positive Tilt: A Beacon of Hope in a Sea of Hate
item.description: Pinterest's unique approach to social media, focusing on positivity and inclusivity, is paying off. The platform has seen a surge in engagement and advertisers are taking notice.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: My 2025 tech resolutions: ditching X, avoiding AI, and more
item.description: So many of 2024’s tech plot twists defied predictions, but we should have seen one thing coming: The industry would once again pivot away from learning from its mistakes.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: Government-imposed internet shutdowns cost over $7 billion in 2024 – and Pakistan was the most affected nation
item.description: While not the most costly year, 2024 saw the highest number of countries affected and blockage hours. Here's what's at stake for 2025.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
item.description: The launch of ChatGPT in November 2022 set off a generative AI gold rush, with companies scrambling to adopt the technology and demonstrate innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Write Python like it’s 2025
item.description: It’s a new year, and we feel fine! 2024 was a breakout year for our favorite programming language, and we have a feeling 2025 will be even bigger.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The cloud architecture renaissance of 2025
item.description: The perfect storm is coming that will force enterprises to rethink their cloud strategy. Cloud architecture will take center stage during 2025. This isn’t just another hype cycle. First, we need to talk about the elephant in the room: generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Your new AirTag has a hidden warning that's just for kids – and it's for the best
item.description: Apple's AirTag is now in compliance with US's Reese's Law, according to the government's consumer product safety watchdog.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Upcoming Tech Conferences 2025 - Event Calendar
item.description: Get ready for hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools! Check out our calendar of upcoming tech conferences in person and virtual.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft claims Edge had a great year, boasts soaring browser user numbers
item.description: Microsoft claims 10 billion GenAI chats happened from within Edge, but browser still lags behind Chrome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Gen AI in 2025: Playtime is over, time to get practical
item.description: Generative AI playtime may be over, as organizations cut down on experimentation and pivot toward achieving business value, with a focus on fewer, more targeted use cases.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intro to VSCode.dev: The IDE in your browser
item.description: The fully realized browser-based IDE has been a long time coming. Ever since the beginning of the cloud revolution, I’ve thought a powerful and flexible remote IDE was just around the corner. I imagined a development tool that could use any virtual machine as a development platform.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The biggest Wi-Fi mistake you didn’t know you were making
item.description: There are many untapped safeguards and tips you can learn to take your Wi-Fi knowledge to the next level, technology expert Kim Komando shares her tricks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI vaporware: 7 products that didn’t materialize in 2024
item.description: Whether to raise money, placate shareholders, or generate positive press, AI’s biggest companies have a habit of announcing advancements that are nowhere near ready to ship.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: The vital role of red teaming in safeguarding AI systems and data
item.description: Companies are feeling pressure to adopt generative AI to stay ahead in a competitive global market. To ensure they adopt responsibly, governing and regulatory bodies across the world are convening to debate and understand the best ways to address AI risk without stifling their innovation edge.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Modern ICAM strategies: Real-time identity management across agencies, partners and at the tactical edge
item.description: Gain insights into the future of ICAM and its role in secure, modern government operations. The post Modern ICAM strategies: Real-time identity management across agencies, partners and at the tactical edge first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The second shoe is about to drop on a big DoD cybersecurity program
item.description: The DoD's big cybersecurity program advanced earlier this month. It's a big rule to carry out if it becomes effective. The post The second shoe is about to drop on a big DoD cybersecurity program first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
